1→#' @import methods
     2→#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix sparseMatrix t
     3→#' @importFrom hdf5r H5File
     4→NULL
     5→
     6→# S4 needs to know 'H5File' is an S3 class from hdf5r
     7→setOldClass("H5File")
     8→
     9→#' H5NeuroVol Class
    10→#'
    11→#' @description
    12→#' A class representing a three-dimensional brain image backed by an HDF5 dataset.
    13→#' \code{H5NeuroVol} objects provide efficient storage and access for large-scale
    14→#' brain imaging data in the HDF5 format.
    15→#'
    16→#' @slot h5obj An instance of class \code{H5File} from the \pkg{hdf5r} package,
    17→#'   representing the underlying HDF5 file containing the brain image data.
    18→#'
    19→#' @details
    20→#' The \code{H5NeuroVol} class inherits a \code{space} slot from
    21→#' \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVol-class}}, which specifies the spatial domain
    22→#' (dimensions, orientation, etc.). Data I/O is performed by reading/writing
    23→#' subsets of the HDF5 dataset, allowing efficient handling of large 3D volumes.
    24→#'
    25→#' @section Inheritance:
    26→#' \code{H5NeuroVol} inherits from:
    27→#' \itemize{
    28→#'   \item \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVol-class}}: Base class for 3D brain volumes
    29→#'   \item \code{\link[neuroim2]{ArrayLike3D-class}}: Interface for 3D array-like operations
    30→#' }
    31→#'
    32→#' @seealso
    33→#' \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVol-class}} for the base 3D brain volume class.
    34→#' \code{\link[hdf5r]{H5File}} for details on HDF5 file objects.
    35→#'
    36→#' @examples
    37→#' \dontrun{
    38→#' if (requireNamespace("neuroim2", quietly = TRUE) &&
    39→#'   requireNamespace("hdf5r", quietly = TRUE) &&
    40→#'   exists("H5NeuroVol", where = "package:fmristore") && # Check if constructor is available
    41→#'   !is.null(fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_H5NeuroVol)) { # Check helper
    42→#'
    43→#'   # Setup: Create a temporary HDF5 file using a helper
    44→#'   # The helper creates a dataset named "data/elements" by default.
    45→#'   temp_h5_path <- NULL
    46→#'   h5_vol <- NULL
    47→#'   tryCatch({
    48→#'     temp_h5_path <- fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_H5NeuroVol(dims = c(5L, 5L, 3L))
    49→#'
    50→#'     # Create an H5NeuroVol object using the constructor
    51→#'     # The constructor defaults to dataset_name = "data/elements" if not specified
    52→#'     # and if a NeuroSpace is not given, it reads it from /space in the HDF5 file.
    53→#'     h5_vol <- fmristore::H5NeuroVol(file_name = temp_h5_path)
    54→#'
    55→#'     print(h5_vol)
    56→#'
    57→#'     # Access a subset of the data
    58→#'     subset_data <- h5_vol[1:2, 1:2, 1]
    59→#'     print(subset_data)
    60→#'
    61→#'   }, error = function(e) {
    62→#'     message("H5NeuroVol example failed: ", e$message)
    63→#'   }, finally = {
    64→#'     # Close HDF5 handle owned by H5NeuroVol object
    65→#'     if (!is.null(h5_vol) && inherits(h5_vol, "H5NeuroVol")) {
    66→#'       try(close(h5_vol), silent = TRUE)
    67→#'     }
    68→#'     # Cleanup temporary file
    69→#'     if (!is.null(temp_h5_path) && file.exists(temp_h5_path)) {
    70→#'       unlink(temp_h5_path)
    71→#'     }
    72→#'   })
    73→#' } else {
    74→#'   message("Skipping H5NeuroVol example: fmristore, neuroim2, hdf5r, or helper not available.")
    75→#' }
    76→#' }
    77→#'
    78→#' @export
    79→#' @rdname H5NeuroVol-class
    80→#' @importClassesFrom neuroim2 ArrayLike3D
    81→#' @importMethodsFrom neuroim2 [
    82→#' @importMethodsFrom neuroim2 linear_access
    83→setClass("H5NeuroVol",
    84→  slots = c(
    85→    h5obj = "H5File"  # underlying HDF5 file handle
    86→  ),
    87→  prototype = list(
    88→    h5obj = NULL # Default to NULL, needs explicit file handle
    89→  ),
    90→  contains = c("NeuroVol", "ArrayLike3D"))
    91→
    92→#' H5NeuroVec Class
    93→#'
    94→#' @description
    95→#' A class representing a four-dimensional brain image backed by an HDF5 file.
    96→#' \code{H5NeuroVec} objects provide efficient storage and access for large-scale
    97→#' 4D neuroimaging data using the HDF5 file format.
    98→#'
    99→#' @slot obj An instance of class \code{H5File} from the \pkg{hdf5r} package,
   100→#'   representing the underlying HDF5 file containing the 4D brain image data.
   101→#'
   102→#' @details
   103→#' \code{H5NeuroVec} inherits a \code{space} slot from
   104→#' \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVec-class}}, defining the 4D dimensions
   105→#' (e.g., \code{x}, \code{y}, \code{z}, \code{time}). Data are stored in an HDF5
   106→#' dataset and accessed on demand.
   107→#'
   108→#' @section Inheritance:
   109→#' \code{H5NeuroVec} inherits from:
   110→#' \itemize{
   111→#'   \item \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVec-class}}: Base class for 4D brain images
   112→#'   \item \code{\link[neuroim2]{ArrayLike4D-class}}: Interface for 4D array-like operations
   113→#' }
   114→#'
   115→#' @seealso
   116→#' \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVec-class}} for the base 4D brain image class.
   117→#' \code{\link{H5NeuroVol-class}} for the 3D counterpart.
   118→#' \code{\link[hdf5r]{H5File}} for details on HDF5 file objects.
   119→#'
   120→#' @examples
   121→#' \dontrun{
   122→#' if (requireNamespace("neuroim2", quietly = TRUE) &&
   123→#'   requireNamespace("hdf5r", quietly = TRUE) &&
   124→#'   exists("H5NeuroVec", where = "package:fmristore") &&
   125→#'   !is.null(fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_H5NeuroVec)) {
   126→#'   # Setup: Create a temporary HDF5 file using a helper
   127→#'   temp_h5_path <- NULL
   128→#'   h5_vec <- NULL
   129→#'   tryCatch({
   130→#'     temp_h5_path <- fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_H5NeuroVec(dims = c(5L, 5L, 3L, 4L))
   131→#'
   132→#'     # Create an H5NeuroVec object using the constructor
   133→#'     # Constructor defaults: dataset_name = "data/elements", space from HDF5 /space group
   134→#'     h5_vec <- fmristore::H5NeuroVec(file_name = temp_h5_path)
   135→#'
   136→#'     print(h5_vec)
   137→#'
   138→#'     # Access a subset of the data
   139→#'     subset_data <- h5_vec[1:2, 1:2, 1, 1:2]
   140→#'     print(subset_data)
   141→#'
   142→#'   }, error = function(e) {
   143→#'     message("H5NeuroVec example failed: ", e$message)
   144→#'   }, finally = {
   145→#'     # Close HDF5 handle owned by H5NeuroVec object
   146→#'     if (!is.null(h5_vec) && inherits(h5_vec, "H5NeuroVec")) {
   147→#'       try(close(h5_vec), silent = TRUE)
   148→#'     }
   149→#'     # Cleanup temporary file
   150→#'     if (!is.null(temp_h5_path) && file.exists(temp_h5_path)) {
   151→#'       unlink(temp_h5_path)
   152→#'     }
   153→#'   })
   154→#' } else {
   155→#'   message("Skipping H5NeuroVec example: fmristore, neuroim2, hdf5r, or helper not available.")
   156→#' }
   157→#' }
   158→#'
   159→#' @export
   160→#' @rdname H5NeuroVec-class
   161→setClass("H5NeuroVec",
   162→  slots = c(
   163→    obj = "H5File",        # underlying HDF5 file handle
   164→    dataset_name = "character" # path to 4D dataset within the file
   165→  ),
   166→  prototype = list(
   167→    obj = NULL,
   168→    dataset_name = "data" # default dataset path
   169→  ),
   170→  contains = c("NeuroVec", "ArrayLike4D"))
   171→
   172→#' H5Format Class
   173→#'
   174→#' @description
   175→#' This class represents the HDF5 (Hierarchical Data Format version 5) file format.
   176→#' It extends \code{FileFormat-class} with HDF5-specific attributes.
   177→#'
   178→#' @seealso \code{\link[neuroim2]{FileFormat-class}}
   179→#'
   180→#' @keywords internal
   181→#' @noRd
   182→setClass("H5Format",
   183→  contains = c("FileFormat"))
   184→
   185→#' H5NeuroVecSource Class
   186→#'
   187→#' @description
   188→#' A class used internally to produce an \code{\linkS4class{H5NeuroVec}} from an HDF5 file.
   189→#'
   190→#' @slot file_name A \code{character} string specifying the name of the HDF5 file.
   191→#'
   192→#' @seealso \code{\link{H5NeuroVec-class}}
   193→#'
   194→#' @keywords internal
   195→#' @noRd
   196→setClass("H5NeuroVecSource",
   197→  representation(file_name = "character"),
   198→  prototype = list(
   199→    file_name = character() # Default to empty character vector
   200→))
   201→
   202→#' LatentNeuroVec Class
   203→#'
   204→#' @description
   205→#' A class that represents a 4-dimensional neuroimaging array using a latent space
   206→#' decomposition. It stores the data as a set of basis functions (dictionary) and
   207→#' a corresponding set of loadings (coefficients), enabling efficient representation
   208→#' and manipulation of high-dimensional data.
   209→#'
   210→#' @slot basis A \code{Matrix} object where each column represents a basis vector
   211→#'   in the latent space.
   212→#' @slot loadings A \code{Matrix} object (often sparse) containing the coefficients
   213→#'   for each basis vector across the spatial dimensions.
   214→#' @slot offset A \code{numeric} vector representing a constant offset term for
   215→#'   each voxel or spatial location.
   216→#' @slot map A \code{IndexLookupVol} object representing the mapping from basis to loadings.
   217→#' @slot label A \code{character} string representing the label for the latent vector.
   218→#'
   219→#' @details
   220→#' \code{LatentNeuroVec} inherits from \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVec-class}}
   221→#' and \code{\link[neuroim2]{AbstractSparseNeuroVec-class}}. The original 4D data
   222→#' can be reconstructed as:
   223→#' \deqn{data[v,t] = \sum_k \bigl(basis[t,k] \times loadings[v,k]\bigr) + offset[v]}.
   224→#' (Note: `v` indexes voxels within the mask).
   225→#'
   226→#' **Important Naming Note:**
   227→#' * In this R object: `@basis` stores temporal components (`nTime x k`), `@loadings` stores spatial components (`nVox x k`).
   228→#' * In the HDF5 spec: `/scans/.../embedding` stores temporal (`nTime x k`), `/basis/basis_matrix` stores spatial (`k x nVox`).
   229→#' The I/O functions handle the mapping and transposition.
   230→#'
   231→#' This approach is especially useful for large datasets where storing the full
   232→#' 4D array is expensive.
   233→#'
   234→#' @section Inheritance:
   235→#' \code{LatentNeuroVec} inherits from:
   236→#' \itemize{
   237→#'   \item \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVec-class}}
   238→#'   \item \code{\link[neuroim2]{AbstractSparseNeuroVec-class}}
   239→#' }
   240→#'
   241→#' @seealso
   242→#' \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVec-class}},
   243→#' \code{\link[neuroim2]{AbstractSparseNeuroVec-class}}.
   244→#'
   245→#' @examples
   246→#' \dontrun{
   247→#' if (requireNamespace("neuroim2", quietly = TRUE) &&
   248→#'   requireNamespace("Matrix", quietly = TRUE) &&
   249→#'   !is.null(fmristore:::create_minimal_LatentNeuroVec)) {
   250→#'   # Create a LatentNeuroVec object using the helper
   251→#'   # The helper creates a mask, basis, and loadings internally.
   252→#'   # It uses new("LatentNeuroVec", ...) after creating constituent parts if not directly calling
   253→#'   # a LatentNeuroVec constructor, or directly calls a constructor.
   254→#'   # Our helper fmristore:::create_minimal_LatentNeuroVec returns a LatentNeuroVec.
   255→#'
   256→#'   latent_vec <- NULL
   257→#'   tryCatch(
   258→#'     {
   259→#'       latent_vec <- fmristore:::create_minimal_LatentNeuroVec(
   260→#'         space_dims = c(5L, 5L, 3L),
   261→#'         n_time = 8L,
   262→#'         n_comp = 2L
   263→#'       )
   264→#'
   265→#'       print(latent_vec)
   266→#'
   267→#'       # Access slots (example)
   268→#'       # print(dim(latent_vec@basis))
   269→#'       # print(dim(latent_vec@loadings))
   270→#'
   271→#'       # Example of accessing data (reconstruction for a voxel would be more complex)
   272→#'       # This class is more about representation; direct element access is usually via methods.
   273→#'       # For example, a method might be `series(latent_vec, vox_indices = c(1,2,3))`
   274→#'       # For a simple demonstration, we can show its dimensions:
   275→#'       print(dim(latent_vec)) # from NeuroVec inheritance
   276→#'
   277→#'     },
   278→#'     error = function(e) {
   279→#'       message("LatentNeuroVec example failed: ", e$message)
   280→#'     })
   281→#'
   282→#' } else {
   283→#'   message("Skipping LatentNeuroVec example: neuroim2, Matrix, or helper not available.")
   284→#' }
   285→#' }
   286→#'
   287→#' @export
   288→#' @rdname LatentNeuroVec-class
   289→setClass("LatentNeuroVec",
   290→  slots = c(
   291→    basis = "Matrix",
   292→    loadings = "Matrix",
   293→    offset = "numeric",
   294→    map = "IndexLookupVol",
   295→    label = "character"
   296→  ),
   297→
   298→  contains = c("NeuroVec", "AbstractSparseNeuroVec"))
   299→
   300→#' LatentNeuroVecSource Class
   301→#'
   302→#' @description
   303→#' A class used intprotoernally to produce a \code{\linkS4class{LatentNeuroVec}} instance.
   304→#'
   305→#' @slot file_name A \code{character} string specifying the file name.
   306→#'
   307→#' @seealso \code{\link{LatentNeuroVec-class}}
   308→#'
   309→#' @keywords internal
   310→#' @noRd
   311→setClass("LatentNeuroVecSource",
   312→  representation(file_name = "character"),
   313→  prototype = list(
   314→    file_name = character() # Default to empty character vector
   315→))
   316→
   317→
   318→#' LabeledVolumeSet Class
   319→#'
   320→#' @description
   321→#' A class representing a multi-volume dataset stored in HDF5, where each volume
   322→#' is labeled (similar to a named 4th dimension). We store:
   323→#' \itemize{
   324→#'   \item a 3D mask
   325→#'   \item a set of label strings
   326→#'   \item for each label, data only at the mask's nonzero entries
   327→#' }
   328→#'
   329→#' This extends \code{NeuroVec}, so it is logically a 4D object with dimension
   330→#' \code{[X, Y, Z, #labels]}.
   331→#'
   332→#' @slot obj An \code{H5File} reference (the file handle).
   333→#' @slot mask A \code{LogicalNeuroVol} of shape [X, Y, Z].
   334→#' @slot labels A \code{character} vector for the volume labels.
   335→#' @slot load_env An \code{environment} storing references for lazy loading.
   336→#'
   337→#' @seealso \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVec-class}}
   338→#'
   339→#' @examples
   340→#' \dontrun{
   341→#' if (requireNamespace("neuroim2", quietly = TRUE) &&
   342→#'   requireNamespace("hdf5r", quietly = TRUE) &&
   343→#'   exists("read_labeled_vec", where = "package:fmristore") &&
   344→#'   !is.null(fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_LabeledVolumeSet)) {
   345→#'   # Setup: Create a temporary HDF5 file suitable for read_labeled_vec
   346→#'   temp_h5_path <- NULL
   347→#'   lvs <- NULL
   348→#'   tryCatch({
   349→#'     temp_h5_path <- fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_LabeledVolumeSet(
   350→#'       vol_dims = c(5L, 4L, 3L),
   351→#'       labels = c("CondA", "CondB"),
   352→#'       num_vols_per_label = 2L
   353→#'     )
   354→#'
   355→#'     # Create a LabeledVolumeSet object using the constructor
   356→#'     lvs <- fmristore::read_labeled_vec(file_name = temp_h5_path)
   357→#'
   358→#'     print(lvs)
   359→#'     print(labels(lvs)) # Access labels
   360→#'
   361→#'     # Access data for a specific label (returns a NeuroVol or similar)
   362→#'     # data_cond_a <- lvs[["CondA"]]
   363→#'     # print(dim(data_cond_a)) # Should be 3D x num_vols_per_label
   364→#'
   365→#'   }, error = function(e) {
   366→#'     message("LabeledVolumeSet example failed: ", e$message)
   367→#'   }, finally = {
   368→#'     # Close HDF5 handle owned by LabeledVolumeSet object
   369→#'     if (!is.null(lvs) && inherits(lvs, "LabeledVolumeSet")) {
   370→#'       try(close(lvs), silent = TRUE)
   371→#'     }
   372→#'     # Cleanup temporary file
   373→#'     if (!is.null(temp_h5_path) && file.exists(temp_h5_path)) {
   374→#'       unlink(temp_h5_path)
   375→#'     }
   376→#'   })
   377→#' } else {
   378→#'   message("Skipping LabeledVolumeSet example: fmristore, neuroim2, hdf5r, or helper not available.")
   379→#' }
   380→#' }
   381→#'
   382→#' @export
   383→setClass("LabeledVolumeSet",
   384→  slots = c(
   385→    obj      = "H5File",          # pointer to open file
   386→    mask     = "LogicalNeuroVol", # 3D mask
   387→    labels   = "character",       # volume (sub-volume) names
   388→    load_env = "environment"     # environment for lazy loading
   389→  ),
   390→  contains = c("NeuroVec"))  # extends NeuroVec
   391→
   392→#' H5ClusteredArray (Virtual Base Class)
   393→#'
   394→#' @description
   395→#' A **virtual** base class for representing clustered neuroimaging data stored in HDF5.
   396→#' It holds the common elements shared across different representations (e.g., full voxel data, summary data).
   397→#' This class is not intended to be instantiated directly.
   398→#'
   399→#' @slot obj An \code{H5File} object representing the open HDF5 file.
   400→#' @slot mask A \code{LogicalNeuroVol} defining the brain mask (shared across runs).
   401→#' @slot clusters A \code{ClusteredNeuroVol} containing cluster assignments (shared across runs).
   402→#' @slot n_voxels An \code{integer} caching the number of voxels in the mask (sum(mask)).
   403→#'
   404→#' @keywords internal
   405→#' @importFrom hdf5r H5File
   406→#' @importFrom methods validObject
   407→#' @importClassesFrom neuroim2 LogicalNeuroVol ClusteredNeuroVol
   408→#' @family H5Cluster
   409→#'
   410→#' @examples
   411→#' # H5ClusteredArray is a virtual class and cannot be directly instantiated.
   412→#' # See its subclasses H5ClusterRun and H5ClusterRunSummary for examples.
   413→#'
   414→#' # You can check if an object inherits from it:
   415→#' # run_object <- H5ClusterRun(...) # Assuming run_object is created
   416→#' # inherits(run_object, "H5ClusteredArray") # Should return TRUE
   417→#'
   418→#' @export
   419→setClass("H5ClusteredArray",
   420→  slots = c(
   421→    obj      = "H5File",
   422→    mask     = "LogicalNeuroVol",
   423→    clusters = "ClusteredNeuroVol",
   424→    n_voxels = "integer"
   425→  ),
   426→  prototype = list(
   427→    obj      = NULL,
   428→    mask     = new("LogicalNeuroVol"),
   429→    clusters = new("ClusteredNeuroVol"),
   430→    n_voxels = NA_integer_
   431→  ),
   432→  validity = function(object) {
   433→    errors <- character()
   434→    # Check 1: n_voxels should match sum(mask), if mask is valid
   435→    mask_space_valid <- !is.null(object@mask) &&
   436→      is(object@mask, "LogicalNeuroVol") &&
   437→      validObject(object@mask@space, test = TRUE)
   438→
   439→    if (mask_space_valid) {
   440→      expected_nvox <- sum(object@mask)
   441→      if (!is.na(object@n_voxels) && object@n_voxels != expected_nvox) {
   442→        errors <- c(errors,
   443→          sprintf("Slot 'n_voxels' (%d) does not match sum(mask) (%d).",
   444→            object@n_voxels, expected_nvox))
   445→      }
   446→      # Check 2: length of cluster vector should match n_voxels (which should match sum(mask))
   447→      if (!is.null(object@clusters) && is(object@clusters, "ClusteredNeuroVol") && length(object@clusters@clusters) > 0) {
   448→        if (length(object@clusters@clusters) != expected_nvox) {
   449→          errors <- c(errors,
   450→            sprintf("Length of clusters@clusters (%d) does not match sum(mask) (%d).",
   451→              length(object@clusters@clusters), expected_nvox))
   452→        }
   453→      }
   454→    }
   455→    # Check 3: Basic type check for H5File handle
   456→    if (!is.null(object@obj) && !inherits(object@obj, "H5File")) {
   457→      errors <- c(errors, "Slot 'obj' must be an H5File object or NULL.")
   458→    }
   459→
   460→    if (length(errors) == 0) TRUE else errors
   461→  },
   462→  contains = "VIRTUAL")
   463→
   464→#' H5ClusterRun Class
   465→#'
   466→#' @description
   467→#' Represents a single "run" or "scan" of full voxel-level clustered time-series data
   468→#' stored in an HDF5 file. It inherits common properties (mask, clusters, file handle)
   469→#' from `H5ClusteredArray` and adds run-specific information.
   470→#'
   471→#' @slot scan_name A \code{character} string identifying the scan (e.g., corresponds to a group under `/scans/`).
   472→#' @slot n_time An \code{integer} specifying the number of time points in this run.
   473→#' @slot compress A \code{logical} indicating if compression was intended or used (metadata).
   474→#' Inherits slots `obj`, `mask`, `clusters`, `n_voxels` from `H5ClusteredArray`.
   475→#'
   476→#' @seealso \code{\link{H5ClusteredArray-class}}, \code{\link{H5ClusterRunSummary-class}}
   477→#' @family H5Cluster
   478→#' @export
   479→setClass("H5ClusterRun",
   480→  slots = c(
   481→    scan_name = "character",
   482→    n_time    = "integer",
   483→    compress  = "logical" # Primarily for metadata/write path
   484→  ),
   485→  prototype = list(
   486→    scan_name = character(),
   487→    n_time = NA_integer_,
   488→    compress = FALSE
   489→    # Inherits prototype for obj, mask, clusters, n_voxels
   490→  ),
   491→  contains = "H5ClusteredArray")
   492→
   493→#' H5ClusterRunSummary Class
   494→#'
   495→#' @description
   496→#' Represents a single "run" or "scan" containing only *summary* time-series data
   497→#' for clusters (e.g., mean signal per cluster) stored in an HDF5 file.
   498→#' It inherits common properties from `H5ClusteredArray` but provides methods suited
   499→#' for accessing summary data (like `as.matrix`) rather than full voxel data.
   500→#'
   501→#' @slot scan_name A \code{character} string identifying the scan.
   502→#' @slot n_time An \code{integer} specifying the number of time points in this run.
   503→#' @slot cluster_names A \code{character} vector providing names for the clusters (columns in summary matrix).
   504→#' @slot cluster_ids An \code{integer} vector of cluster IDs corresponding to `cluster_names`.
   505→#' @slot summary_dset A \code{character} string giving the name of the summary dataset within the run's HDF5 group (e.g., "summary_data").
   506→#' Inherits slots `obj`, `mask`, `n_voxels` from `H5ClusteredArray`.
   507→#' Note: The `clusters` slot inherited from `H5ClusteredArray` might be NULL or contain the map for reference, but voxel-level access methods are typically disabled/error.
   508→#'
   509→#' @seealso \code{\link{H5ClusteredArray-class}}, \code{\link{H5ClusterRun-class}}
   510→#' @family H5Cluster
   511→#' @export
   512→setClass("H5ClusterRunSummary",
   513→  slots = c(
   514→    scan_name     = "character",
   515→    n_time        = "integer",
   516→    cluster_names = "character",
   517→    cluster_ids   = "integer",
   518→    summary_dset  = "character"
   519→  ),
   520→  prototype = list(
   521→    scan_name     = character(),
   522→    n_time        = NA_integer_,
   523→    cluster_names = character(),
   524→    cluster_ids   = integer(),
   525→    summary_dset  = "summary_data"
   526→  ),
   527→  contains = "H5ClusteredArray")
   528→
   529→#' H5ClusterExperiment Class
   530→#'
   531→#' @description
   532→#' Represents a collection of clustered neuroimaging runs (scans) stored within a single HDF5 file.
   533→#' It acts as a container for `H5ClusterRun` and/or `H5ClusterRunSummary` objects,
   534→#' along with associated metadata.
   535→#'
   536→#' This class facilitates managing multiple runs that share the same mask and cluster definitions.
   537→#'
   538→#' @slot runs A `list` where each element is an object inheriting from `H5ClusteredArray`
   539→#'   (typically `H5ClusterRun` or `H5ClusterRunSummary`).
   540→#' @slot scan_metadata A `list` containing metadata for each scan in the `runs` list.
   541→#' @slot cluster_metadata A `data.frame` containing metadata associated with the clusters.
   542→#'
   543→#' @seealso \code{\link{H5ClusterRun-class}}, \code{\link{H5ClusterRunSummary-class}}
   544→#' @family H5Cluster
   545→#' @export
   546→setClass("H5ClusterExperiment",
   547→  slots = c(
   548→    runs             = "list",
   549→    scan_metadata    = "list",
   550→    cluster_metadata = "data.frame"
   551→  ),
   552→  prototype = list(
   553→    runs             = list(),
   554→    scan_metadata    = list(),
   555→    cluster_metadata = data.frame()
   556→  ),
   557→  validity = function(object) {
   558→    errors <- character()
   559→    n_runs <- length(object@runs)
   560→
   561→    # Check 1: All elements in 'runs' list must inherit from H5ClusteredArray
   562→    if (n_runs > 0) {
   563→      is_valid_run <- vapply(object@runs, inherits, logical(1),
   564→        what = "H5ClusteredArray")
   565→      if (!all(is_valid_run)) {
   566→        invalid_indices <- which(!is_valid_run)
   567→        errors <- c(errors,
   568→          paste0("Elements at indices ",
   569→            paste(invalid_indices, collapse = ", "),
   570→            " in the 'runs' list do not inherit from H5ClusteredArray."))
   571→        # Stop further checks if basic type is wrong
   572→        return(errors)
   573→      }
   574→    }
   575→
   576→    # Check 2: Ensure scan_metadata has same length as runs if not empty
   577→    if (length(object@scan_metadata) > 0 && length(object@scan_metadata) != n_runs) {
   578→      errors <- c(errors,
   579→        sprintf("Length of 'scan_metadata' (%d) does not match length of 'runs' list (%d).",
   580→          length(object@scan_metadata), n_runs))
   581→    }
   582→
   583→    # Check 3: If multiple runs, verify they share the same H5File, mask, and clusters
   584→    if (n_runs > 1) {
   585→      first_run <- object@runs[[1]]
   586→      # Check H5 File (using filename as a robust check)
   587→      first_filename <- tryCatch(first_run@obj$get_filename(), error = function(e) NA_character_)
   588→      if (is.na(first_filename)) {
   589→        errors <- c(errors, "Could not get HDF5 filename from the first run object.")
   590→      }
   591→
   592→      for (i in 2:n_runs) {
   593→        current_run <- object@runs[[i]]
   594→        # Check H5 File consistency
   595→        current_filename <- tryCatch(current_run@obj$get_filename(), error = function(e) NA_character_)
   596→        if (is.na(current_filename) || !identical(first_filename, current_filename)) {
   597→          errors <- c(errors,
   598→            sprintf("Run %d uses a different HDF5 file ('%s') than Run 1 ('%s').",
   599→              i, current_filename, first_filename))
   600→        }
   601→        # Check mask consistency (using identical to check for same object in memory)
   602→        if (!identical(first_run@mask, current_run@mask)) {
   603→          errors <- c(errors, sprintf("Run %d has a different mask object than Run 1.", i))
   604→        }
   605→        # Check clusters consistency (using identical)
   606→        # Need to handle NULL case for H5ClusterRunSummary
   607→        if (!identical(first_run@clusters, current_run@clusters)) {
   608→          # Allow comparison if both are NULL (summary runs might have NULL clusters)
   609→          if (!(is.null(first_run@clusters) && is.null(current_run@clusters))) {
   610→            errors <- c(errors, sprintf("Run %d has a different clusters object than Run 1.", i))
   611→          }
   612→        }
   613→      }
   614→    }
   615→
   616→    if (length(errors) == 0) TRUE else errors
   617→  }
   618→  # Does not contain H5ClusteredArray
   619→)
   620→
   621→#' H5NeuroVecSeq Class
   622→#'
   623→#' @description
   624→#' Represents a collection of 4D scans stored in a single HDF5 file.
   625→#' Each scan is accessed lazily via an internal \code{H5NeuroVec}.
   626→#'
   627→#' @slot obj An \code{H5File} handle to the underlying HDF5 file.
   628→#' @slot vecs A list of \code{H5NeuroVec} objects, one per scan.
   629→#' @export
   630→setClass("H5NeuroVecSeq",
   631→  slots = c(
   632→    obj  = "H5File",
   633→    vecs = "list"
   634→  ),
   635→  prototype = list(
   636→    obj  = NULL,
   637→    vecs = list()
   638→  ))
   639→
   640→#' @seealso \code{\link{H5ClusterRun-class}}, \code{\link{H5ClusterRunSummary-class}}
   641→#' @family H5Cluster
   642→#'
   643→#' @examples
   644→#' \dontrun{
   645→#' if (requireNamespace("neuroim2", quietly = TRUE) &&
   646→#'   requireNamespace("hdf5r", quietly = TRUE) &&
   647→#'   exists("H5ClusterExperiment", where = "package:fmristore") &&
   648→#'   !is.null(fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_H5ClusterExperiment)) {
   649→#'
   650→#'   temp_exp_file <- NULL
   651→#'   exp <- NULL
   652→#'   tryCatch({
   653→#'     # 1. Create an HDF5 file for an experiment containing runs
   654→#'     temp_exp_file <- fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_H5ClusterExperiment()
   655→#'
   656→#'     # 2. Load the experiment using the constructor
   657→#'     exp <- fmristore::H5ClusterExperiment(file_path = temp_exp_file)
   658→#'
   659→#'     # 3. Show the experiment object
   660→#'     print(exp)
   661→#'     print(names(runs(exp))) # Show the names of the runs it loaded
   662→#'
   663→#'   }, error = function(e) {
   664→#'     message("H5ClusterExperiment example failed: ", e$message)
   665→#'   }, finally = {
   666→#'     # Close H5ClusterExperiment handle (closes underlying file)
   667→#'     if (!is.null(exp)) try(close(exp), silent = TRUE)
   668→#'     # Cleanup temporary file
   669→#'     if (!is.null(temp_exp_file) && file.exists(temp_exp_file)) {
   670→#'       unlink(temp_exp_file)
   671→#'     }
   672→#'   })
   673→#' } else {
   674→#'   message("Skipping H5ClusterExperiment example: dependencies or helper not available.")
   675→#' }
   676→#' }
   677→#'
   678→#' @export
   679→setClass("H5ClusterExperiment",
   680→  slots = c(
   681→    runs             = "list",
   682→    scan_metadata    = "list",
   683→    cluster_metadata = "data.frame"
   684→  ),
   685→  prototype = list(
   686→    runs             = list(),
   687→    scan_metadata    = list(),
   688→    cluster_metadata = data.frame()
   689→  ),
   690→  validity = function(object) {
   691→    errors <- character()
   692→    n_runs <- length(object@runs)
   693→
   694→    # Check 1: All elements in 'runs' list must inherit from H5ClusteredArray
   695→    if (n_runs > 0) {
   696→      is_valid_run <- vapply(object@runs, inherits, logical(1),
   697→        what = "H5ClusteredArray")
   698→      if (!all(is_valid_run)) {
   699→        invalid_indices <- which(!is_valid_run)
   700→        errors <- c(errors,
   701→          paste0("Elements at indices ",
   702→            paste(invalid_indices, collapse = ", "),
   703→            " in the 'runs' list do not inherit from H5ClusteredArray."))
   704→        # Stop further checks if basic type is wrong
   705→        return(errors)
   706→      }
   707→    }
   708→
   709→    # Check 2: Ensure scan_metadata has same length as runs if not empty
   710→    if (length(object@scan_metadata) > 0 && length(object@scan_metadata) != n_runs) {
   711→      errors <- c(errors,
   712→        sprintf("Length of 'scan_metadata' (%d) does not match length of 'runs' list (%d).",
   713→          length(object@scan_metadata), n_runs))
   714→    }
   715→
   716→    # Check 3: If multiple runs, verify they share the same H5File, mask, and clusters
   717→    if (n_runs > 1) {
   718→      first_run <- object@runs[[1]]
   719→      # Check H5 File (using filename as a robust check)
   720→      first_filename <- tryCatch(first_run@obj$get_filename(), error = function(e) NA_character_)
   721→      if (is.na(first_filename)) {
   722→        errors <- c(errors, "Could not get HDF5 filename from the first run object.")
   723→      }
   724→
   725→      for (i in 2:n_runs) {
   726→        current_run <- object@runs[[i]]
   727→        # Check H5 File consistency
   728→        current_filename <- tryCatch(current_run@obj$get_filename(), error = function(e) NA_character_)
   729→        if (is.na(current_filename) || !identical(first_filename, current_filename)) {
   730→          errors <- c(errors,
   731→            sprintf("Run %d uses a different HDF5 file ('%s') than Run 1 ('%s').",
   732→              i, current_filename, first_filename))
   733→        }
   734→        # Check mask consistency (using identical to check for same object in memory)
   735→        if (!identical(first_run@mask, current_run@mask)) {
   736→          errors <- c(errors, sprintf("Run %d has a different mask object than Run 1.", i))
   737→        }
   738→        # Check clusters consistency (using identical)
   739→        # Need to handle NULL case for H5ClusterRunSummary
   740→        if (!identical(first_run@clusters, current_run@clusters)) {
   741→          # Allow comparison if both are NULL (summary runs might have NULL clusters)
   742→          if (!(is.null(first_run@clusters) && is.null(current_run@clusters))) {
   743→            errors <- c(errors, sprintf("Run %d has a different clusters object than Run 1.", i))
   744→          }
   745→        }
   746→      }
   747→    }
   748→
   749→    if (length(errors) == 0) TRUE else errors
   750→  }
   751→  # Does not contain H5ClusteredArray
   752→)
   753→

#' @import methods
#' @importFrom Matrix Matrix sparseMatrix t
#' @importFrom hdf5r H5File
NULL

# S4 needs to know 'H5File' is an S3 class from hdf5r
setOldClass("H5File")

#' H5NeuroVol Class
#'
#' @description
#' A class representing a three-dimensional brain image backed by an HDF5 dataset.
#' \code{H5NeuroVol} objects provide efficient storage and access for large-scale
#' brain imaging data in the HDF5 format.
#'
#' @slot h5obj An instance of class \code{H5File} from the \pkg{hdf5r} package,
#'   representing the underlying HDF5 file containing the brain image data.
#'
#' @details
#' The \code{H5NeuroVol} class inherits a \code{space} slot from
#' \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVol-class}}, which specifies the spatial domain
#' (dimensions, orientation, etc.). Data I/O is performed by reading/writing
#' subsets of the HDF5 dataset, allowing efficient handling of large 3D volumes.
#'
#' @section Inheritance:
#' \code{H5NeuroVol} inherits from:
#' \itemize{
#'   \item \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVol-class}}: Base class for 3D brain volumes
#'   \item \code{\link[neuroim2]{ArrayLike3D-class}}: Interface for 3D array-like operations
#' }
#'
#' @seealso
#' \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVol-class}} for the base 3D brain volume class.
#' \code{\link[hdf5r]{H5File}} for details on HDF5 file objects.
#'
#' @examples
#' \dontrun{
#' if (requireNamespace("neuroim2", quietly = TRUE) &&
#'   requireNamespace("hdf5r", quietly = TRUE) &&
#'   exists("H5NeuroVol", where = "package:fmristore") && # Check if constructor is available
#'   !is.null(fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_H5NeuroVol)) { # Check helper
#'
#'   # Setup: Create a temporary HDF5 file using a helper
#'   # The helper creates a dataset named "data/elements" by default.
#'   temp_h5_path <- NULL
#'   h5_vol <- NULL
#'   tryCatch({
#'     temp_h5_path <- fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_H5NeuroVol(dims = c(5L, 5L, 3L))
#'
#'     # Create an H5NeuroVol object using the constructor
#'     # The constructor defaults to dataset_name = "data/elements" if not specified
#'     # and if a NeuroSpace is not given, it reads it from /space in the HDF5 file.
#'     h5_vol <- fmristore::H5NeuroVol(file_name = temp_h5_path)
#'
#'     print(h5_vol)
#'
#'     # Access a subset of the data
#'     subset_data <- h5_vol[1:2, 1:2, 1]
#'     print(subset_data)
#'
#'   }, error = function(e) {
#'     message("H5NeuroVol example failed: ", e$message)
#'   }, finally = {
#'     # Close HDF5 handle owned by H5NeuroVol object
#'     if (!is.null(h5_vol) && inherits(h5_vol, "H5NeuroVol")) {
#'       try(close(h5_vol), silent = TRUE)
#'     }
#'     # Cleanup temporary file
#'     if (!is.null(temp_h5_path) && file.exists(temp_h5_path)) {
#'       unlink(temp_h5_path)
#'     }
#'   })
#' } else {
#'   message("Skipping H5NeuroVol example: fmristore, neuroim2, hdf5r, or helper not available.")
#' }
#' }
#'
#' @export
#' @rdname H5NeuroVol-class
#' @importClassesFrom neuroim2 ArrayLike3D
#' @importMethodsFrom neuroim2 [
#' @importMethodsFrom neuroim2 linear_access
setClass("H5NeuroVol",
  slots = c(
    h5obj = "H5File"  # underlying HDF5 file handle
  ),
  prototype = list(
    h5obj = NULL # Default to NULL, needs explicit file handle
  ),
  contains = c("NeuroVol", "ArrayLike3D"))

#' H5NeuroVec Class
#'
#' @description
#' A class representing a four-dimensional brain image backed by an HDF5 file.
#' \code{H5NeuroVec} objects provide efficient storage and access for large-scale
#' 4D neuroimaging data using the HDF5 file format.
#'
#' @slot obj An instance of class \code{H5File} from the \pkg{hdf5r} package,
#'   representing the underlying HDF5 file containing the 4D brain image data.
#'
#' @details
#' \code{H5NeuroVec} inherits a \code{space} slot from
#' \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVec-class}}, defining the 4D dimensions
#' (e.g., \code{x}, \code{y}, \code{z}, \code{time}). Data are stored in an HDF5
#' dataset and accessed on demand.
#'
#' @section Inheritance:
#' \code{H5NeuroVec} inherits from:
#' \itemize{
#'   \item \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVec-class}}: Base class for 4D brain images
#'   \item \code{\link[neuroim2]{ArrayLike4D-class}}: Interface for 4D array-like operations
#' }
#'
#' @seealso
#' \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVec-class}} for the base 4D brain image class.
#' \code{\link{H5NeuroVol-class}} for the 3D counterpart.
#' \code{\link[hdf5r]{H5File}} for details on HDF5 file objects.
#'
#' @examples
#' \dontrun{
#' if (requireNamespace("neuroim2", quietly = TRUE) &&
#'   requireNamespace("hdf5r", quietly = TRUE) &&
#'   exists("H5NeuroVec", where = "package:fmristore") &&
#'   !is.null(fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_H5NeuroVec)) {
#'   # Setup: Create a temporary HDF5 file using a helper
#'   temp_h5_path <- NULL
#'   h5_vec <- NULL
#'   tryCatch({
#'     temp_h5_path <- fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_H5NeuroVec(dims = c(5L, 5L, 3L, 4L))
#'
#'     # Create an H5NeuroVec object using the constructor
#'     # Constructor defaults: dataset_name = "data/elements", space from HDF5 /space group
#'     h5_vec <- fmristore::H5NeuroVec(file_name = temp_h5_path)
#'
#'     print(h5_vec)
#'
#'     # Access a subset of the data
#'     subset_data <- h5_vec[1:2, 1:2, 1, 1:2]
#'     print(subset_data)
#'
#'   }, error = function(e) {
#'     message("H5NeuroVec example failed: ", e$message)
#'   }, finally = {
#'     # Close HDF5 handle owned by H5NeuroVec object
#'     if (!is.null(h5_vec) && inherits(h5_vec, "H5NeuroVec")) {
#'       try(close(h5_vec), silent = TRUE)
#'     }
#'     # Cleanup temporary file
#'     if (!is.null(temp_h5_path) && file.exists(temp_h5_path)) {
#'       unlink(temp_h5_path)
#'     }
#'   })
#' } else {
#'   message("Skipping H5NeuroVec example: fmristore, neuroim2, hdf5r, or helper not available.")
#' }
#' }
#'
#' @export
#' @rdname H5NeuroVec-class
setClass("H5NeuroVec",
  slots = c(
    obj = "H5File",        # underlying HDF5 file handle
    dataset_name = "character" # path to 4D dataset within the file
  ),
  prototype = list(
    obj = NULL,
    dataset_name = "data" # default dataset path
  ),
  contains = c("NeuroVec", "ArrayLike4D"))

#' H5Format Class
#'
#' @description
#' This class represents the HDF5 (Hierarchical Data Format version 5) file format.
#' It extends \code{FileFormat-class} with HDF5-specific attributes.
#'
#' @seealso \code{\link[neuroim2]{FileFormat-class}}
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
setClass("H5Format",
  contains = c("FileFormat"))

#' H5NeuroVecSource Class
#'
#' @description
#' A class used internally to produce an \code{\linkS4class{H5NeuroVec}} from an HDF5 file.
#'
#' @slot file_name A \code{character} string specifying the name of the HDF5 file.
#'
#' @seealso \code{\link{H5NeuroVec-class}}
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
setClass("H5NeuroVecSource",
  representation(file_name = "character"),
  prototype = list(
    file_name = character() # Default to empty character vector
))

#' LatentNeuroVec Class
#'
#' @description
#' A class that represents a 4-dimensional neuroimaging array using a latent space
#' decomposition. It stores the data as a set of basis functions (dictionary) and
#' a corresponding set of loadings (coefficients), enabling efficient representation
#' and manipulation of high-dimensional data.
#'
#' @slot basis A \code{Matrix} object where each column represents a basis vector
#'   in the latent space.
#' @slot loadings A \code{Matrix} object (often sparse) containing the coefficients
#'   for each basis vector across the spatial dimensions.
#' @slot offset A \code{numeric} vector representing a constant offset term for
#'   each voxel or spatial location.
#' @slot map A \code{IndexLookupVol} object representing the mapping from basis to loadings.
#' @slot label A \code{character} string representing the label for the latent vector.
#'
#' @details
#' \code{LatentNeuroVec} inherits from \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVec-class}}
#' and \code{\link[neuroim2]{AbstractSparseNeuroVec-class}}. The original 4D data
#' can be reconstructed as:
#' \deqn{data[v,t] = \sum_k \bigl(basis[t,k] \times loadings[v,k]\bigr) + offset[v]}.
#' (Note: `v` indexes voxels within the mask).
#'
#' **Important Naming Note:**
#' * In this R object: `@basis` stores temporal components (`nTime x k`), `@loadings` stores spatial components (`nVox x k`).
#' * In the HDF5 spec: `/scans/.../embedding` stores temporal (`nTime x k`), `/basis/basis_matrix` stores spatial (`k x nVox`).
#' The I/O functions handle the mapping and transposition.
#'
#' This approach is especially useful for large datasets where storing the full
#' 4D array is expensive.
#'
#' @section Inheritance:
#' \code{LatentNeuroVec} inherits from:
#' \itemize{
#'   \item \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVec-class}}
#'   \item \code{\link[neuroim2]{AbstractSparseNeuroVec-class}}
#' }
#'
#' @seealso
#' \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVec-class}},
#' \code{\link[neuroim2]{AbstractSparseNeuroVec-class}}.
#'
#' @examples
#' \dontrun{
#' if (requireNamespace("neuroim2", quietly = TRUE) &&
#'   requireNamespace("Matrix", quietly = TRUE) &&
#'   !is.null(fmristore:::create_minimal_LatentNeuroVec)) {
#'   # Create a LatentNeuroVec object using the helper
#'   # The helper creates a mask, basis, and loadings internally.
#'   # It uses new("LatentNeuroVec", ...) after creating constituent parts if not directly calling
#'   # a LatentNeuroVec constructor, or directly calls a constructor.
#'   # Our helper fmristore:::create_minimal_LatentNeuroVec returns a LatentNeuroVec.
#'
#'   latent_vec <- NULL
#'   tryCatch(
#'     {
#'       latent_vec <- fmristore:::create_minimal_LatentNeuroVec(
#'         space_dims = c(5L, 5L, 3L),
#'         n_time = 8L,
#'         n_comp = 2L
#'       )
#'
#'       print(latent_vec)
#'
#'       # Access slots (example)
#'       # print(dim(latent_vec@basis))
#'       # print(dim(latent_vec@loadings))
#'
#'       # Example of accessing data (reconstruction for a voxel would be more complex)
#'       # This class is more about representation; direct element access is usually via methods.
#'       # For example, a method might be `series(latent_vec, vox_indices = c(1,2,3))`
#'       # For a simple demonstration, we can show its dimensions:
#'       print(dim(latent_vec)) # from NeuroVec inheritance
#'
#'     },
#'     error = function(e) {
#'       message("LatentNeuroVec example failed: ", e$message)
#'     })
#'
#' } else {
#'   message("Skipping LatentNeuroVec example: neuroim2, Matrix, or helper not available.")
#' }
#' }
#'
#' @export
#' @rdname LatentNeuroVec-class
setClass("LatentNeuroVec",
  slots = c(
    basis = "Matrix",
    loadings = "Matrix",
    offset = "numeric",
    map = "IndexLookupVol",
    label = "character"
  ),

  contains = c("NeuroVec", "AbstractSparseNeuroVec"))

#' LatentNeuroVecSource Class
#'
#' @description
#' A class used intprotoernally to produce a \code{\linkS4class{LatentNeuroVec}} instance.
#'
#' @slot file_name A \code{character} string specifying the file name.
#'
#' @seealso \code{\link{LatentNeuroVec-class}}
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
setClass("LatentNeuroVecSource",
  representation(file_name = "character"),
  prototype = list(
    file_name = character() # Default to empty character vector
))


#' LabeledVolumeSet Class
#'
#' @description
#' A class representing a multi-volume dataset stored in HDF5, where each volume
#' is labeled (similar to a named 4th dimension). We store:
#' \itemize{
#'   \item a 3D mask
#'   \item a set of label strings
#'   \item for each label, data only at the mask's nonzero entries
#' }
#'
#' This extends \code{NeuroVec}, so it is logically a 4D object with dimension
#' \code{[X, Y, Z, #labels]}.
#'
#' @slot obj An \code{H5File} reference (the file handle).
#' @slot mask A \code{LogicalNeuroVol} of shape [X, Y, Z].
#' @slot labels A \code{character} vector for the volume labels.
#' @slot load_env An \code{environment} storing references for lazy loading.
#'
#' @seealso \code{\link[neuroim2]{NeuroVec-class}}
#'
#' @examples
#' \dontrun{
#' if (requireNamespace("neuroim2", quietly = TRUE) &&
#'   requireNamespace("hdf5r", quietly = TRUE) &&
#'   exists("read_labeled_vec", where = "package:fmristore") &&
#'   !is.null(fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_LabeledVolumeSet)) {
#'   # Setup: Create a temporary HDF5 file suitable for read_labeled_vec
#'   temp_h5_path <- NULL
#'   lvs <- NULL
#'   tryCatch({
#'     temp_h5_path <- fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_LabeledVolumeSet(
#'       vol_dims = c(5L, 4L, 3L),
#'       labels = c("CondA", "CondB"),
#'       num_vols_per_label = 2L
#'     )
#'
#'     # Create a LabeledVolumeSet object using the constructor
#'     lvs <- fmristore::read_labeled_vec(file_name = temp_h5_path)
#'
#'     print(lvs)
#'     print(labels(lvs)) # Access labels
#'
#'     # Access data for a specific label (returns a NeuroVol or similar)
#'     # data_cond_a <- lvs[["CondA"]]
#'     # print(dim(data_cond_a)) # Should be 3D x num_vols_per_label
#'
#'   }, error = function(e) {
#'     message("LabeledVolumeSet example failed: ", e$message)
#'   }, finally = {
#'     # Close HDF5 handle owned by LabeledVolumeSet object
#'     if (!is.null(lvs) && inherits(lvs, "LabeledVolumeSet")) {
#'       try(close(lvs), silent = TRUE)
#'     }
#'     # Cleanup temporary file
#'     if (!is.null(temp_h5_path) && file.exists(temp_h5_path)) {
#'       unlink(temp_h5_path)
#'     }
#'   })
#' } else {
#'   message("Skipping LabeledVolumeSet example: fmristore, neuroim2, hdf5r, or helper not available.")
#' }
#' }
#'
#' @export
setClass("LabeledVolumeSet",
  slots = c(
    obj      = "H5File",          # pointer to open file
    mask     = "LogicalNeuroVol", # 3D mask
    labels   = "character",       # volume (sub-volume) names
    load_env = "environment"     # environment for lazy loading
  ),
  contains = c("NeuroVec"))  # extends NeuroVec

#' H5ClusteredArray (Virtual Base Class)
#'
#' @description
#' A **virtual** base class for representing clustered neuroimaging data stored in HDF5.
#' It holds the common elements shared across different representations (e.g., full voxel data, summary data).
#' This class is not intended to be instantiated directly.
#'
#' @slot obj An \code{H5File} object representing the open HDF5 file.
#' @slot mask A \code{LogicalNeuroVol} defining the brain mask (shared across runs).
#' @slot clusters A \code{ClusteredNeuroVol} containing cluster assignments (shared across runs).
#' @slot n_voxels An \code{integer} caching the number of voxels in the mask (sum(mask)).
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @importFrom hdf5r H5File
#' @importFrom methods validObject
#' @importClassesFrom neuroim2 LogicalNeuroVol ClusteredNeuroVol
#' @family H5Cluster
#'
#' @examples
#' # H5ClusteredArray is a virtual class and cannot be directly instantiated.
#' # See its subclasses H5ClusterRun and H5ClusterRunSummary for examples.
#'
#' # You can check if an object inherits from it:
#' # run_object <- H5ClusterRun(...) # Assuming run_object is created
#' # inherits(run_object, "H5ClusteredArray") # Should return TRUE
#'
#' @export
setClass("H5ClusteredArray",
  slots = c(
    obj      = "H5File",
    mask     = "LogicalNeuroVol",
    clusters = "ClusteredNeuroVol",
    n_voxels = "integer"
  ),
  prototype = list(
    obj      = NULL,
    mask     = new("LogicalNeuroVol"),
    clusters = new("ClusteredNeuroVol"),
    n_voxels = NA_integer_
  ),
  validity = function(object) {
    errors <- character()
    # Check 1: n_voxels should match sum(mask), if mask is valid
    mask_space_valid <- !is.null(object@mask) &&
      is(object@mask, "LogicalNeuroVol") &&
      validObject(object@mask@space, test = TRUE)

    if (mask_space_valid) {
      expected_nvox <- sum(object@mask)
      if (!is.na(object@n_voxels) && object@n_voxels != expected_nvox) {
        errors <- c(errors,
          sprintf("Slot 'n_voxels' (%d) does not match sum(mask) (%d).",
            object@n_voxels, expected_nvox))
      }
      # Check 2: length of cluster vector should match n_voxels (which should match sum(mask))
      if (!is.null(object@clusters) && is(object@clusters, "ClusteredNeuroVol") && length(object@clusters@clusters) > 0) {
        if (length(object@clusters@clusters) != expected_nvox) {
          errors <- c(errors,
            sprintf("Length of clusters@clusters (%d) does not match sum(mask) (%d).",
              length(object@clusters@clusters), expected_nvox))
        }
      }
    }
    # Check 3: Basic type check for H5File handle
    if (!is.null(object@obj) && !inherits(object@obj, "H5File")) {
      errors <- c(errors, "Slot 'obj' must be an H5File object or NULL.")
    }

    if (length(errors) == 0) TRUE else errors
  },
  contains = "VIRTUAL")

#' H5ClusterRun Class
#'
#' @description
#' Represents a single "run" or "scan" of full voxel-level clustered time-series data
#' stored in an HDF5 file. It inherits common properties (mask, clusters, file handle)
#' from `H5ClusteredArray` and adds run-specific information.
#'
#' @slot scan_name A \code{character} string identifying the scan (e.g., corresponds to a group under `/scans/`).
#' @slot n_time An \code{integer} specifying the number of time points in this run.
#' @slot compress A \code{logical} indicating if compression was intended or used (metadata).
#' Inherits slots `obj`, `mask`, `clusters`, `n_voxels` from `H5ClusteredArray`.
#'
#' @seealso \code{\link{H5ClusteredArray-class}}, \code{\link{H5ClusterRunSummary-class}}
#' @family H5Cluster
#' @export
setClass("H5ClusterRun",
  slots = c(
    scan_name = "character",
    n_time    = "integer",
    compress  = "logical" # Primarily for metadata/write path
  ),
  prototype = list(
    scan_name = character(),
    n_time = NA_integer_,
    compress = FALSE
    # Inherits prototype for obj, mask, clusters, n_voxels
  ),
  contains = "H5ClusteredArray")

#' H5ClusterRunSummary Class
#'
#' @description
#' Represents a single "run" or "scan" containing only *summary* time-series data
#' for clusters (e.g., mean signal per cluster) stored in an HDF5 file.
#' It inherits common properties from `H5ClusteredArray` but provides methods suited
#' for accessing summary data (like `as.matrix`) rather than full voxel data.
#'
#' @slot scan_name A \code{character} string identifying the scan.
#' @slot n_time An \code{integer} specifying the number of time points in this run.
#' @slot cluster_names A \code{character} vector providing names for the clusters (columns in summary matrix).
#' @slot cluster_ids An \code{integer} vector of cluster IDs corresponding to `cluster_names`.
#' @slot summary_dset A \code{character} string giving the name of the summary dataset within the run's HDF5 group (e.g., "summary_data").
#' Inherits slots `obj`, `mask`, `n_voxels` from `H5ClusteredArray`.
#' Note: The `clusters` slot inherited from `H5ClusteredArray` might be NULL or contain the map for reference, but voxel-level access methods are typically disabled/error.
#'
#' @seealso \code{\link{H5ClusteredArray-class}}, \code{\link{H5ClusterRun-class}}
#' @family H5Cluster
#' @export
setClass("H5ClusterRunSummary",
  slots = c(
    scan_name     = "character",
    n_time        = "integer",
    cluster_names = "character",
    cluster_ids   = "integer",
    summary_dset  = "character"
  ),
  prototype = list(
    scan_name     = character(),
    n_time        = NA_integer_,
    cluster_names = character(),
    cluster_ids   = integer(),
    summary_dset  = "summary_data"
  ),
  contains = "H5ClusteredArray")

#' H5ClusterExperiment Class
#'
#' @description
#' Represents a collection of clustered neuroimaging runs (scans) stored within a single HDF5 file.
#' It acts as a container for `H5ClusterRun` and/or `H5ClusterRunSummary` objects,
#' along with associated metadata.
#'
#' This class facilitates managing multiple runs that share the same mask and cluster definitions.
#'
#' @slot runs A `list` where each element is an object inheriting from `H5ClusteredArray`
#'   (typically `H5ClusterRun` or `H5ClusterRunSummary`).
#' @slot scan_metadata A `list` containing metadata for each scan in the `runs` list.
#' @slot cluster_metadata A `data.frame` containing metadata associated with the clusters.
#'
#' @seealso \code{\link{H5ClusterRun-class}}, \code{\link{H5ClusterRunSummary-class}}
#' @family H5Cluster
#' @export
setClass("H5ClusterExperiment",
  slots = c(
    runs             = "list",
    scan_metadata    = "list",
    cluster_metadata = "data.frame"
  ),
  prototype = list(
    runs             = list(),
    scan_metadata    = list(),
    cluster_metadata = data.frame()
  ),
  validity = function(object) {
    errors <- character()
    n_runs <- length(object@runs)

    # Check 1: All elements in 'runs' list must inherit from H5ClusteredArray
    if (n_runs > 0) {
      is_valid_run <- vapply(object@runs, inherits, logical(1),
        what = "H5ClusteredArray")
      if (!all(is_valid_run)) {
        invalid_indices <- which(!is_valid_run)
        errors <- c(errors,
          paste0("Elements at indices ",
            paste(invalid_indices, collapse = ", "),
            " in the 'runs' list do not inherit from H5ClusteredArray."))
        # Stop further checks if basic type is wrong
        return(errors)
      }
    }

    # Check 2: Ensure scan_metadata has same length as runs if not empty
    if (length(object@scan_metadata) > 0 && length(object@scan_metadata) != n_runs) {
      errors <- c(errors,
        sprintf("Length of 'scan_metadata' (%d) does not match length of 'runs' list (%d).",
          length(object@scan_metadata), n_runs))
    }

    # Check 3: If multiple runs, verify they share the same H5File, mask, and clusters
    if (n_runs > 1) {
      first_run <- object@runs[[1]]
      # Check H5 File (using filename as a robust check)
      first_filename <- tryCatch(first_run@obj$get_filename(), error = function(e) NA_character_)
      if (is.na(first_filename)) {
        errors <- c(errors, "Could not get HDF5 filename from the first run object.")
      }

      for (i in 2:n_runs) {
        current_run <- object@runs[[i]]
        # Check H5 File consistency
        current_filename <- tryCatch(current_run@obj$get_filename(), error = function(e) NA_character_)
        if (is.na(current_filename) || !identical(first_filename, current_filename)) {
          errors <- c(errors,
            sprintf("Run %d uses a different HDF5 file ('%s') than Run 1 ('%s').",
              i, current_filename, first_filename))
        }
        # Check mask consistency (using identical to check for same object in memory)
        if (!identical(first_run@mask, current_run@mask)) {
          errors <- c(errors, sprintf("Run %d has a different mask object than Run 1.", i))
        }
        # Check clusters consistency (using identical)
        # Need to handle NULL case for H5ClusterRunSummary
        if (!identical(first_run@clusters, current_run@clusters)) {
          # Allow comparison if both are NULL (summary runs might have NULL clusters)
          if (!(is.null(first_run@clusters) && is.null(current_run@clusters))) {
            errors <- c(errors, sprintf("Run %d has a different clusters object than Run 1.", i))
          }
        }
      }
    }

    if (length(errors) == 0) TRUE else errors
  }
  # Does not contain H5ClusteredArray
)

#' H5NeuroVecSeq Class
#'
#' @description
#' Represents a collection of 4D scans stored in a single HDF5 file.
#' Each scan is accessed lazily via an internal \code{H5NeuroVec}.
#'
#' @slot obj An \code{H5File} handle to the underlying HDF5 file.
#' @slot vecs A list of \code{H5NeuroVec} objects, one per scan.
#' @export
setClass("H5NeuroVecSeq",
  slots = c(
    obj  = "H5File",
    vecs = "list"
  ),
  prototype = list(
    obj  = NULL,
    vecs = list()
  ))

#' @seealso \code{\link{H5ClusterRun-class}}, \code{\link{H5ClusterRunSummary-class}}
#' @family H5Cluster
#'
#' @examples
#' \dontrun{
#' if (requireNamespace("neuroim2", quietly = TRUE) &&
#'   requireNamespace("hdf5r", quietly = TRUE) &&
#'   exists("H5ClusterExperiment", where = "package:fmristore") &&
#'   !is.null(fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_H5ClusterExperiment)) {
#'
#'   temp_exp_file <- NULL
#'   exp <- NULL
#'   tryCatch({
#'     # 1. Create an HDF5 file for an experiment containing runs
#'     temp_exp_file <- fmristore:::create_minimal_h5_for_H5ClusterExperiment()
#'
#'     # 2. Load the experiment using the constructor
#'     exp <- fmristore::H5ClusterExperiment(file_path = temp_exp_file)
#'
#'     # 3. Show the experiment object
#'     print(exp)
#'     print(names(runs(exp))) # Show the names of the runs it loaded
#'
#'   }, error = function(e) {
#'     message("H5ClusterExperiment example failed: ", e$message)
#'   }, finally = {
#'     # Close H5ClusterExperiment handle (closes underlying file)
#'     if (!is.null(exp)) try(close(exp), silent = TRUE)
#'     # Cleanup temporary file
#'     if (!is.null(temp_exp_file) && file.exists(temp_exp_file)) {
#'       unlink(temp_exp_file)
#'     }
#'   })
#' } else {
#'   message("Skipping H5ClusterExperiment example: dependencies or helper not available.")
#' }
#' }
#'
#' @export
setClass("H5ClusterExperiment",
  slots = c(
    runs             = "list",
    scan_metadata    = "list",
    cluster_metadata = "data.frame"
  ),
  prototype = list(
    runs             = list(),
    scan_metadata    = list(),
    cluster_metadata = data.frame()
  ),
  validity = function(object) {
    errors <- character()
    n_runs <- length(object@runs)

    # Check 1: All elements in 'runs' list must inherit from H5ClusteredArray
    if (n_runs > 0) {
      is_valid_run <- vapply(object@runs, inherits, logical(1),
        what = "H5ClusteredArray")
      if (!all(is_valid_run)) {
        invalid_indices <- which(!is_valid_run)
        errors <- c(errors,
          paste0("Elements at indices ",
            paste(invalid_indices, collapse = ", "),
            " in the 'runs' list do not inherit from H5ClusteredArray."))
        # Stop further checks if basic type is wrong
        return(errors)
      }
    }

    # Check 2: Ensure scan_metadata has same length as runs if not empty
    if (length(object@scan_metadata) > 0 && length(object@scan_metadata) != n_runs) {
      errors <- c(errors,
        sprintf("Length of 'scan_metadata' (%d) does not match length of 'runs' list (%d).",
          length(object@scan_metadata), n_runs))
    }

    # Check 3: If multiple runs, verify they share the same H5File, mask, and clusters
    if (n_runs > 1) {
      first_run <- object@runs[[1]]
      # Check H5 File (using filename as a robust check)
      first_filename <- tryCatch(first_run@obj$get_filename(), error = function(e) NA_character_)
      if (is.na(first_filename)) {
        errors <- c(errors, "Could not get HDF5 filename from the first run object.")
      }

      for (i in 2:n_runs) {
        current_run <- object@runs[[i]]
        # Check H5 File consistency
        current_filename <- tryCatch(current_run@obj$get_filename(), error = function(e) NA_character_)
        if (is.na(current_filename) || !identical(first_filename, current_filename)) {
          errors <- c(errors,
            sprintf("Run %d uses a different HDF5 file ('%s') than Run 1 ('%s').",
              i, current_filename, first_filename))
        }
        # Check mask consistency (using identical to check for same object in memory)
        if (!identical(first_run@mask, current_run@mask)) {
          errors <- c(errors, sprintf("Run %d has a different mask object than Run 1.", i))
        }
        # Check clusters consistency (using identical)
        # Need to handle NULL case for H5ClusterRunSummary
        if (!identical(first_run@clusters, current_run@clusters)) {
          # Allow comparison if both are NULL (summary runs might have NULL clusters)
          if (!(is.null(first_run@clusters) && is.null(current_run@clusters))) {
            errors <- c(errors, sprintf("Run %d has a different clusters object than Run 1.", i))
          }
        }
      }
    }

    if (length(errors) == 0) TRUE else errors
  }
  # Does not contain H5ClusteredArray
)

content.text:
Based on the all_class.R file, here are all the S4 classes defined:

1. **H5NeuroVol** - 3D brain image backed by HDF5 dataset
2. **H5NeuroVec** - 4D brain image backed by HDF5 file  
3. **H5Format** - HDF5 file format representation
4. **H5NeuroVecSource** - Internal class for producing H5NeuroVec from HDF5 file
5. **LatentNeuroVec** - 4D neuroimaging array using latent space decomposition
6. **LatentNeuroVecSource** - Internal class for producing LatentNeuroVec
7. **LabeledVolumeSet** - Multi-volume dataset with labeled volumes in HDF5
8. **H5ClusteredArray** - Virtual base class for clustered neuroimaging data
9. **H5ClusterRun** - Single run of full voxel-level clustered time-series data
10. **H5ClusterRunSummary** - Single run with summary time-series data for clusters
11. **H5ClusterExperiment** - Collection of clustered neuroimaging runs (duplicate definition)
12. **H5NeuroVecSeq** - Collection of 4D scans in single HDF5 file

Note: There's a duplicate definition of `H5ClusterExperiment` class at lines 546-619 and 679-752 in the file.

message.content: ok, H5ClusterExperiment, H5ClusterRunSummary and H5ClusterRun have somewhat dubious names. can you summarize what each one actually does, briefly.

content.text:
Based on the class definitions, here's what each one actually does:

**H5ClusterExperiment**: A container that holds multiple runs/scans from the same experiment. It's like a collection or dataset that manages several related brain scans that share the same mask and cluster definitions.

**H5ClusterRun**: Represents a single brain scan with full voxel-level time-series data organized by clusters. It stores the complete time-series for every voxel within each cluster.

**H5ClusterRunSummary**: Represents a single brain scan but only stores summary statistics (like mean signal) for each cluster, not the individual voxel data. Much more memory efficient than the full version.

The naming is indeed confusing because:
- "Experiment" suggests the whole study, but it's really just a multi-run container
- "Run" is ambiguous - could mean execution or a single scan session
- The distinction between full vs summary data isn't clear from the names alone

Better names might be something like:
- `H5MultiRunContainer` or `H5ScanCollection` 
- `H5FullClusterScan` 
- `H5SummaryClusterScan`

message.content: H5ClusterExperiment: H5ParcellatedMultiScan ; H5ClusterRun : H5ParcellatedScan ; H5ClusterRunSummary : H5ParcellatedScanSummary ; to rename, we need to do it carefully: provide ffedback on names; then formulate a script that will very carefully rename usages.

content.text:
The proposed names are much clearer! "Parcellated" accurately describes the clustering/grouping aspect, and the hierarchy makes sense:

**Feedback on proposed names:**
- `H5ParcellatedMultiScan` - Perfect. "Multi" clearly indicates multiple scans, "Parcellated" describes the clustering
- `H5ParcellatedScan` - Good. Single scan with parcellation/clustering
- `H5ParcellatedScanSummary` - Excellent. The "Summary" suffix clearly indicates reduced/aggregated data

These names are much more descriptive than the current "Cluster" terminology and follow a logical naming convention.

content.content:
540→#' @slot scan_metadata A `list` containing metadata for each scan in the `runs` list.
   541→#' @slot cluster_metadata A `data.frame` containing metadata associated with the clusters.
   542→#'
   543→#' @seealso \code{\link{H5ClusterRun-class}}, \code{\link{H5ClusterRunSummary-class}}
   544→#' @family H5Cluster
   545→#' @export
   546→setClass("H5ClusterExperiment",
   547→  slots = c(
   548→    runs             = "list",
   549→    scan_metadata    = "list",
   550→    cluster_metadata = "data.frame"
   551→  ),
   552→  prototype = list(
   553→    runs             = list(),
   554→    scan_metadata    = list(),
   555→    cluster_metadata = data.frame()
   556→  ),
   557→  validity = function(object) {
   558→    errors <- character()
   559→    n_runs <- length(object@runs)
   560→
   561→    # Check 1: All elements in 'runs' list must inherit from H5ClusteredArray
   562→    if (n_runs > 0) {
   563→      is_valid_run <- vapply(object@runs, inherits, logical(1),
   564→        what = "H5ClusteredArray")
   565→      if (!all(is_valid_run)) {
   566→        invalid_indices <- which(!is_valid_run)
   567→        errors <- c(errors,
   568→          paste0("Elements at indices ",
   569→            paste(invalid_indices, collapse = ", "),
   570→            " in the 'runs' list do not inherit from H5ClusteredArray."))
   571→        # Stop further checks if basic type is wrong
   572→        return(errors)
   573→      }
   574→    }
   575→
   576→    # Check 2: Ensure scan_metadata has same length as runs if not empty
   577→    if (length(object@scan_metadata) > 0 && length(object@scan_metadata) != n_runs) {
   578→      errors <- c(errors,
   579→        sprintf("Length of 'scan_metadata' (%d) does not match length of 'runs' list (%d).",
   580→          length(object@scan_metadata), n_runs))
   581→    }
   582→
   583→    # Check 3: If multiple runs, verify they share the same H5File, mask, and clusters
   584→    if (n_runs > 1) {
   585→      first_run <- object@runs[[1]]
   586→      # Check H5 File (using filename as a robust check)
   587→      first_filename <- tryCatch(first_run@obj$get_filename(), error = function(e) NA_character_)
   588→      if (is.na(first_filename)) {
   589→        errors <- c(errors, "Could not get HDF5 filename from the first run object.")
   590→      }
   591→
   592→      for (i in 2:n_runs) {
   593→        current_run <- object@runs[[i]]
   594→        # Check H5 File consistency
   595→        current_filename <- tryCatch(current_run@obj$get_filename(), error = function(e) NA_character_)
   596→        if (is.na(current_filename) || !identical(first_filename, current_filename)) {
   597→          errors <- c(errors,
   598→            sprintf("Run %d uses a different HDF5 file ('%s') than Run 1 ('%s').",
   599→              i, current_filename, first_filename))
   600→        }
   601→        # Check mask consistency (using identical to check for same object in memory)
   602→        if (!identical(first_run@mask, current_run@mask)) {

#' @slot scan_metadata A `list` containing metadata for each scan in the `runs` list.
#' @slot cluster_metadata A `data.frame` containing metadata associated with the clusters.
#'
#' @seealso \code{\link{H5ClusterRun-class}}, \code{\link{H5ClusterRunSummary-class}}
#' @family H5Cluster
#' @export
setClass("H5ClusterExperiment",
  slots = c(
    runs             = "list",
    scan_metadata    = "list",
    cluster_metadata = "data.frame"
  ),
  prototype = list(
    runs             = list(),
    scan_metadata    = list(),
    cluster_metadata = data.frame()
  ),
  validity = function(object) {
    errors <- character()
    n_runs <- length(object@runs)

    # Check 1: All elements in 'runs' list must inherit from H5ClusteredArray
    if (n_runs > 0) {
      is_valid_run <- vapply(object@runs, inherits, logical(1),
        what = "H5ClusteredArray")
      if (!all(is_valid_run)) {
        invalid_indices <- which(!is_valid_run)
        errors <- c(errors,
          paste0("Elements at indices ",
            paste(invalid_indices, collapse = ", "),
            " in the 'runs' list do not inherit from H5ClusteredArray."))
        # Stop further checks if basic type is wrong
        return(errors)
      }
    }

    # Check 2: Ensure scan_metadata has same length as runs if not empty
    if (length(object@scan_metadata) > 0 && length(object@scan_metadata) != n_runs) {
      errors <- c(errors,
        sprintf("Length of 'scan_metadata' (%d) does not match length of 'runs' list (%d).",
          length(object@scan_metadata), n_runs))
    }

    # Check 3: If multiple runs, verify they share the same H5File, mask, and clusters
    if (n_runs > 1) {
      first_run <- object@runs[[1]]
      # Check H5 File (using filename as a robust check)
      first_filename <- tryCatch(first_run@obj$get_filename(), error = function(e) NA_character_)
      if (is.na(first_filename)) {
        errors <- c(errors, "Could not get HDF5 filename from the first run object.")
      }

      for (i in 2:n_runs) {
        current_run <- object@runs[[i]]
        # Check H5 File consistency
        current_filename <- tryCatch(current_run@obj$get_filename(), error = function(e) NA_character_)
        if (is.na(current_filename) || !identical(first_filename, current_filename)) {
          errors <- c(errors,
            sprintf("Run %d uses a different HDF5 file ('%s') than Run 1 ('%s').",
              i, current_filename, first_filename))
        }
        # Check mask consistency (using identical to check for same object in memory)
        if (!identical(first_run@mask, current_run@mask)) {
          errors <- c(errors, sprintf("Run %d has a different mask object than Run 1.", i))
        }
        # Check clusters consistency (using identical)
        # Need to handle NULL case for H5ClusterRunSummary
        if (!identical(first_run@clusters, current_run@clusters)) {
          # Allow comparison if both are NULL (summary runs might have NULL clusters)
          if (!(is.null(first_run@clusters) && is.null(current_run@clusters))) {
            errors <- c(errors, sprintf("Run %d has a different clusters object than Run 1.", i))
          }
        }
      }
    }

    if (length(errors) == 0) TRUE else errors
  }
  # Does not contain H5ClusteredArray
)

#' H5NeuroVecSeq Class
#'
#' @description
#' Represents a collection of 4D scans stored in a single HDF5 file.
#' Each scan is accessed lazily via an internal \code{H5NeuroVec}.
#'
#' @slot obj An \code{H5File} handle to the underlying HDF5 file.
#' @slot vecs A list of \code{H5NeuroVec} objects, one per scan.
#' @export
setClass("H5NeuroVecSeq",
  slots = c(
    obj  = "H5File",
    vecs = "list"
  ),
  prototype = list(
    obj  = NULL,
    vecs = list()
  ))
